risks.description: devicePixelRatio scaling adds backing-store logic that all future visual modules must respect
risks.mitigation: DPR logic is centralized in scene.ts and resize.ts; future Three.js renderer will handle its own DPR via setPixelRatio

risks.description: jsdom canvas limitations may produce false-positive tests if getContext is mocked too loosely
risks.mitigation: Plan acknowledges this; keep mocks minimal and verify real behavior via build + manual smoke test

architecturalImpact.reasoning: US-002 is a leaf-level UI shell story. It touches only visual bootstrap modules and CSS—no new dependencies, no API surface changes, no data flow alterations. The 2D placeholder canvas will be replaced by Three.js in a later story, so the main lasting artifacts are the CSS reset, the resize handler pattern, and the test harness.

planQuality.completeness: high — all four acceptance criteria are explicitly mapped to steps
planQuality.stepOrdering: correct — audit existing code before writing tests, run build last
planQuality.scopeCreep: none — Three.js, audio, and UI overlays are explicitly out of scope
missingConsiderations: Plan should confirm that canvas element uses id or data attribute so future modules can query it if needed
No mention of cleanup/teardown in resize listener — acceptable for a single-page app but worth noting

suggestions: Ensure the resize handler is exported as a named function so it can be detached in tests to avoid listener leaks between test runs
Consider using visualViewport API as a future enhancement note — not needed now but more accurate on mobile
